---
title: "Codex Academic Workflow Guide"
subtitle: "Pragmatic setup for slides, papers, and analysis"
author: "Project Maintainer"
date: "2026-03-05"
date-modified: last-modified
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-location: left
    number-sections: true
    theme:
      - cosmo
      - custom.scss
    code-copy: true
    code-overflow: wrap
    smooth-scroll: true
    self-contained: true
---

# Overview

This guide documents a Codex-first workflow for academic project execution.

The operating model is simple:

1. Plan non-trivial tasks.
2. Execute with small, verifiable edits.
3. Verify outputs (compile/render/tests).
4. Record decisions and learnings.

# Required Files

- `AGENTS.md`: collaboration contract and operating rules
- `MEMORY.md`: persistent lessons (`[LEARN:*]` entries)
- `templates/`: reusable documents for specs and logs

# Setup

## 1. Clone

```bash
git clone <your-fork-url> my-project
cd my-project
```

## 2. Initialize project rules

- Fill placeholders in `AGENTS.md`
- Confirm quality thresholds
- Confirm source-of-truth policy for artifacts

## 3. Start first task

Use a concrete prompt, for example:

> Read `AGENTS.md` and propose a plan to create Lecture 1 slides with Beamer and a matching Quarto version.

# Standard Workflows

## Plan-first for non-trivial work

Use when task is ambiguous, spans multiple files, or has high impact.

Deliverables:
- Plan document in `quality_reports/plans/`
- Clear verification checklist

## Verification

For each artifact type:

- `.tex`: compile and check warnings/errors
- `.qmd`: render and inspect output in `docs/`
- `.R`: run script and verify expected outputs

## Session logging

For substantial sessions, maintain `quality_reports/session_logs/YYYY-MM-DD_topic.md`.

# Quality Gates

- `>=80`: commit-ready
- `>=90`: PR/deploy-ready
- `<80`: blocked until critical issues are fixed

# Templates

- `templates/requirements-spec.md`
- `templates/session-log.md`
- `templates/quality-report.md`
- `templates/skill-template.md`

# Migration Note

This repository is Codex-only. Claude-specific configuration and hooks are intentionally excluded.
